description: The vibes kill-criteria memo: both criteria are moods ('sentiment deteriorates', 'stops feeling durable') that no kill monitor could ever evaluate against DCP tables. The CIO template requires observable conditions; falsifiability theater must fail the observability metric.

memo.thesis: The committee rejects AVGO for new capital: the momentum family failed its decision gates on real data and the validated cross-sectional family does not select the name, so there is no covered edge to deploy.
kill_criteria: Sentiment around the name deteriorates materially and the street sours on the story
The bull narrative no longer feels durable to the committee
memo.dissent: A rejection built on gate verdicts alone ignores that validation lags the tape: persistent relative strength through a flat market is the profile the fund's own point-in-time machinery selects for, and waiting may surrender the whole entry.

evidence: AVGO daily closes (EODHD vendor bars, split-adjusted): latest close 172.35 on 2026-07-10, previous close 170.10, 20 sessions ago 158.32. Window: 60 sessions ending 2026-07-10.

Quant validation record as of 2026-07-10: family momentum-v1 FAILED decision gates on real data (null-model p 0.830, deflated Sharpe negative, 0 of 4 walk-forward folds passed); family xsmom-pit holds a decision-grade PASS on the S&P 500 point-in-time universe (4 of 4 folds, null p 0.000, deflated Sharpe 0.998). AVGO is not in the current xsmom-pit winner decile; no validated strategy currently covers AVGO.
